feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Applicable where 100 Kbps mode is implemented; uses SCL and SDA.
test_steps: Configure the core for 100 Kbps I2C operation.
Apply a normal-mode I2C transfer using SCL and SDA.
Observe that the transfer operates at the configured 100 Kbps mode.
expected_results: The core supports 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation.
The transfer completes in the normal mode setting.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can directly support 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation, where implemented.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Normal: 100Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Applicable where correct hardware is included; uses SCL and SDA.
test_steps: Configure the core for 400 Kbps I2C operation.
Apply a fast-mode I2C transfer using SCL and SDA.
Observe that the transfer operates at the configured 400 Kbps mode.
expected_results: The core supports 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation.
The transfer completes in the fast mode setting.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can support 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation, where correct hardware is included.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Fast: 400Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that the core presents a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.test_constraints: Uses wb_clk_i, wb_rst_i, wb_adr_i, wb_dat_i, wb_dat_o, wb_we_i, wb_stb_i, wb_cyc_i, wb_ack_o, wb_inta_o.
test_steps: Apply WISHBONE bus activity to the core interface.
Observe the presence and use of the listed WISHBONE signals.
Confirm the interface operates as WISHBONE Classic compliant behavior.
expected_results: The core exposes a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
The listed WISHBONE signals are available on the interface.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core shall implement a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.requirement_text: The core features a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.

feature_list.test_description: Verify that each WISHBONE access completes in two clock cycles.
feature_list.test_constraints: Uses wb_clk_i, wb_cyc_i, wb_stb_i, and wb_ack_o.
test_steps: Initiate a WISHBONE access by asserting wb_cyc_i and wb_stb_i.
Count clock cycles on wb_clk_i until wb_ack_o is asserted.
Repeat for a representative access.
expected_results: Each WISHBONE access takes 2 clock cycles.
wb_ack_o is asserted on the second clock cycle of the access.
feature_list.requirement_description: Each WISHBONE access must take two clock cycles.

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset operation when the reset level is configured active high.
test_steps: Configure ARST_LVL to active high.
Assert arst_i to the active-high level.
Observe core reset behavior.
expected_results: The asynchronous reset responds to the active-high level.
The core enters reset when arst_i is asserted high.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset operation when the reset level is configured active low.
test_steps: Configure ARST_LVL to active low.
Assert arst_i to the active-low level.
Observe core reset behavior.
expected_results: The asynchronous reset responds to the active-low level.
The core enters reset when arst_i is asserted low.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify SCL external connection signals are exposed for open-drain bus connection.
test_steps: Inspect the external connections for SCL-related signals.
Apply a representative SCL pad input/output condition.
Observe the SCL pad input, output, and output-enable signals.
expected_results: scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, and scl_pad_oe are present.
The SCL pad signals support external bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable

feature_list.test_description: Verify SDA external connection signals are exposed for open-drain bus connection.
test_steps: Inspect the external connections for SDA-related signals.
Apply a representative SDA pad input/output condition.
Observe the SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals.
expected_results: sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe are present.
The SDA pad signals support external bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable
